text:
The Olympics is hotting up as the US and China fight for top spot. The two heavyweight nations have pulled away from the rest of the pack and are both gunning for first place when the Games wraps up on Sunday.

Australia and hosts France are the best of the rest heading into the weekend but Great Britain are hot on their heels as they look to secure a top-five finish.

There are plenty of medal events remaining for Team GB to surge up the table and Express Sport will bring you all the big wins throughout the final few days...

It's silver for GB in the taekwondo +80kg final.

Caden Cunningham beaten loses to Arian Salimi of Iran despite winning the first round.

Lin Yu-Ting haswon boxing gold.

One of two boxers caught up in a gender row in Paris alongside Imane Khelif, just like the Algerian last now, the Taiwanese boxer wins her weight category.

She beats Julia Szeremeta to win gold in the women's 57kg boxing.

It's a brilliant silver for Great Britain next up.

Kate Shortman and Izzy Thorpe produce history with Team GB's first ever medal in artistic swimming.

It's bronze for GB in the men's and women's 4x400m relays.

Superb from Alex Haydock-Wilson, Matt Hudson-Smith, Lewis Davey and Charlie Dobson.

And that effort is matched by Nicole Yeargin, Victoria Ohuruogu, Amber Anning and Laviai Nielsen. It's a British record from the four women.

USA take gold in both events. They've got 34 athletic medals in Paris.

GB have got 10, which is only less than the Americans.

Brilliant effort from Georgia Bell as she takes bronze in the women's 1500m final.

Laura Muir just misses out in fifth.

Tokyo 2020 silver medallist Muir said to theBBC: "I can't complain but congrats to Georgia - absolutely amazing."

Bell adds:

Bell said: "I don't know if I have ever been this happy. That was an absolutely crazy race. I am over the moon, an Olympic medallist."

Another bronze for Team GB as Noah Williams pulls off a perfect dive of 94.35 in the men's 10m platform final.

Erin McNeice has agonisingly missed out on an Olympic medal in the sport climbing - women's boulder and lead final.

The Team GB star produced a very strong performance, but has to settle for fifth.

Slovenia's Janja Garnbret has taken the gold.

Imane Khelif cruised to the gold medal in the women's 66kg event.

Khelif overcame Yang Liu of China in the final, producing yet another dominant display amid the gender row that has engulfed the Olympics.

Katarina Johnson-Thompson produced a brilliant effort in the final 800m event of the heptathlon, but she couldn't quite beat Nafi Thiam by the eight-and-a-half deficit margin she needed to claim gold.

The Team GB star wins silver, her first Olympic medal.

After the women's silver, the men's 4x100m relay team have clinched bronze in a dramatic race after a shocker from the USA.

Canada win gold ahead of South Africa in second.

Jeremiah Azu, Louie Hinchliffe. Nethaneel Mitchell-Blake and Zharnel Hughes will leave the Olympics with a medal!

Team GB have collected another silver, this time in the 4x100m women's relay final.

Dina Asher-Smith, Imani-Lara Lansiquot, Amy Hunt and Daryll Neita were pipped to gold by the USA after a poor final changeover.

Team GB duo Elinor Barker and Neah Evans have secured a silver medal in the women's madison following a brilliant finale to the race.

Italy have claimed gold while bronze has gone to the Netherlands.

It's a 14th GB goldthanks to climber Toby Robertsin the men's boulder and lead competition.

It is the country's first ever sport climbing medal at the Olympics.

His score of 155.2 sees him trump Japan's Anraku Sorato.

Some brilliant drama in the athletics at the Stade de France tonight.

100m winner Noah Lyles was only fast enough for bronze in the 200m and has since been diagnosed withCovid.

Botswana's Letsile Tebogo was instead an impressive winner.

GB's Katarina Johnson-Thompson also leads the heptathlon at the end of day one.

Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone has just blown everyone away in the women's 400m hurdle final with a new world record time of 50.37.

She was miles clear of Dutch rival Femke Bol who was only good enough for bronze.

Elsewhere, the USA snap up two more golds as Grant Holloway wins the men's 110m hurdles final and Tara Davis-Woodhall becomes the new long jump champion.

It's bronze for Emma Finucane in the women's keirin final as Katy Marchant finishes fourth without a medal.

That moves Great Britain up to 51 medals at the 2024 Olympics.

Team GB finally have their 13th gold - they've been waiting a few days for it!

Ellie Aldridge delivers it in the women's kite final.

Heartbreak for Matthew Hudson-Smith as American Quincy Hall just pips him at the line.

Hudson-Smith had led for 399m of the men's 400m final and posted 43.44 - a European record.

But Hall's 43.40 takes the USA to another gold.

Team GB will end day 12 on 12 golds having come so close to being on 14.

Another medal in the velodrome for Team GB.

In the women's team pursuit bronze medal race, GB pip Italy to the line.

It's silver for Team GB in the men's team pursuit.

GB are now up to 47 medals overall with 12 golds and 16 silvers.

Australia are on 17 golds after winning this one.

Gabby Thomas has claimed gold for USA in the 200m final.

There was heartbreak for Team GB though, as Dina Asher-Smith and Daryll Neita finished fourth and fifth respectively.

It looked as if Josh Kerr was going to win gold in the 1500m final after leading going down the straight.

Howevver, USA's Cole Hocker pipped him at the line to smash the Olympic record with a time of 3:27:65.

Meanwhile, Kerr's bitter rival Jakob Ingebrigtsen finished outside the medal spots.

Team GB have picked up silver in the men's team sprint final in track cycling.

The trio of Ed Lowe, Hamish Turnbull and Jack Carlin were beaten by the Dutch, who are dominant in the event.

Sky Brown, at the age of 16, has won another bronze for Team GB in the women's skateboarding park final.

Another superb display from the teenager after battling a shoulder injury in the build-up to the final.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

The first gold of the day goes to Germany.

Christian Kukuk wins the individual jumping final while riding Checker 47.

Steve Guerdat, of Switzerland, takes second. Dutch star Maikel van der Vleuten lands bronze.

Here are Team GB's medal hopes for today;

9am - Equestrian - Jumping Individual final (Team GB hopes: Ben Maher, Harry Charles and Scott Brash)

2pm - Diving - Women's 10m Platform Final (Team GB hopes: Andrea Spendolini-Sirieix)

2.43pm - Sailing - Men's Dinghy Medal Race (Team GB hope: Michael Beckett)

4.30pm - Skateboarding - Women's individual final (Team GB hopes: Sky Brown and Lola Tambling)

7.07pm - Cycling Track - Men's Team Sprint Finals (Team GB hopes: Jack Carlin, Edward Lowe and Hamish Turnbull)

7.50pm - Athletics - Men's 1,500m final (Team GB hopes: Josh Kerr and Neil Gourley)

8.14pm - Athletics - Women's 3000m steeplechase final (Team GB hopes: Elizabeth Bird)

8.40pm - Athletics - Women's 200m final (Team GB hopes: Darryl Neita and Dina Asher-Smith)
